methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Sicona Battery Technologies is a Series A-stage company in the Climate & Energy sector that has raised over $22M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of nine private companies and one public equity (LNZA), with primary focus on three same-sector, same-stage peers: Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy.

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given available data, anchors valuation at $150M. This reflects the median valuation across the three selected comparables, which range from $110M (Steady Energy) to $200M (Graphyte). The broader peer set includes later-stage companies such as TAE Technologies and Redwood Materials, which provide directional context but are less directly comparable due to stage differences. No DCF or revenue multiple analysis is supported by the provided data.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $128M to $172M, centered on $150M. Key uncertainties include the limited sample size of one method (three peers) and the absence of financial metrics—revenue, burn rate, or capital efficiency—that would strengthen conviction in peer selection or identify material differences between Sicona and comparables. Stage-appropriate peer data is constrained, and public market data is minimal. Additional financial performance data and specificity on technology differentiation would be required to narrow this range materially.
